public_reputation: Panim noble whose prestige is articulated through contemplative gardens and refined ritual hospitality.
appearance: Lady Elyss carries the marks of lady in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries the habits of a lady whose work is visible through daily procedure, gatekeeping, and the practical pressures of Elysian Grove.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of lady in and around Elysian Grove, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Elysian Grove while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Lady Elyss can draw the party into a practical problem centered on Elysian Grove.
Lady Elyss becomes useful when the party needs access, explanation, or relief tied to the work of a lady.

support_scope_notes: Created from the estate and daily-life infrastructure execution patch to make polity-native estate operations usable at table scale.
public_signal: In public, Lady Elyss is readable as a lady tied to Elysian Grove.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Elysian Grove: role-relevant halls, counters, corridors, docks, archives, shrines, workshops, gates, or administrative rooms

first_impression: Lady Elyss presents as someone shaped by the duties of a lady and the daily pressures of Elysian Grove.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a lady, and proof that strangers will not make Elysian Grove harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Lady Elyss refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a lady too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Lady Elyss can open local doors, explain expectations, and redirect pressure before it hardens.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Lady Elyss adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Lady Elyss always knows more about Elysian Grove than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to lady duties in Elysian Grove.
social_texture: Lady Elyss speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Elysian Grove, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Lady touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Lady Elyss when the party needs a socially grounded face for Elysian Grove: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
